facts.statement: Danaher is classified under SIC code 'Industrial Instruments For Measurement, Display, and Control' and trades on the NYSE under the ticker DHR.
Danaher reported total revenue of $23.89 billion for FY2025.
Danaher reported net income of $4.764 billion for FY2025.
Danaher generated $7.164 billion in operating cash flow for FY2025.
Danaher spent $1.503 billion on research and development in FY2025.
As of FY2025, Danaher had stockholders' equity of $49.543 billion and long-term debt of $15.5 billion.

Danaher operates through three reportable segments: Biotechnology, Life Sciences, and Diagnostics, with revenue tracked across geographic regions including North America, Western Europe, Other Developed Markets, and High Growth Markets.
Danaher tracks recurring and nonrecurring revenue separately for each of its Biotechnology, Life Sciences, and Diagnostics segments.

In February 2026, Danaher entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Masimo Corporation, a specialty diagnostics provider of pulse oximetry and patient monitoring systems, for approximately $9.9 billion including assumed indebtedness and net of acquired cash.
In 2025, Danaher returned approximately $4.0 billion to shareholders through stock buybacks and dividends.
Danaher invested approximately $1.2 billion in capital expenditures in 2025 to support future core growth.
Danaher completed the acquisition of Abcam on December 6, 2023, with the 10-K disclosing related fair value adjustments to inventory and settlement of share-based payment awards.
Danaher separated its Environmental & Applied Solutions segment as Veralto Corporation on September 29, 2023, with associated debt transferred and equity awards adjusted.
Rainer M. Blair serves as President and Chief Executive Officer of Danaher and has been a director since 2020.
Matthew R. McGrew serves as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer of Danaher.
Steven M. Rales serves as Chairman of the Board and Mitchell P. Rales serves as Chairman of the Executive Committee; the Chairman and CEO positions are separate.
Danaher's board has eleven director nominees for the 2026 annual meeting, with average director nominee tenure declining by more than 11% over the past seven years.

Danaher has no supermajority voting requirements, no shareholder rights plan, provides proxy access, allows shareholders owning 25% or more to call special meetings, and all directors are elected annually by majority vote.

Total pretax stock-based compensation expense was $298 million in 2025, with $168 million of unrecognized RSU/PSU compensation cost and $150 million of unrecognized stock option compensation cost expected to be recognized over approximately two years.

Danaher's executive compensation program features multi-year vesting for stock options, three-year performance periods plus two-year holding periods for PSUs, no tax gross-ups, no dividends on unvested equity, and no single-trigger change-of-control benefits.

Danaher has multiple series of senior unsecured notes outstanding with maturities ranging from 2026 through 2051, denominated in USD, CHF, EUR, and JPY, and maintains a Third Amended and Restated Multiyear Credit Facility dated August 11, 2023.
Danaher reports revenue by country including the United States, China, and other countries, with geographic concentration risk disclosures for both sales and property, plant, and equipment.

Danaher and its subsidiaries have made no political contributions in the last decade and have no intention of contributing Danaher funds for political purposes; the company was ranked as a First Tier company in the 2025 CPA-Zicklin Index.
Ernst & Young LLP (PCAOB ID: 00042) serves as Danaher's independent registered public accounting firm, with shareholders asked to ratify this selection for 2026.
